title: context-status lifecycle metrics special-case stock kinds (phase/requirements/verification); custom kinds get only generic summaries
description: contextState() (context-sdk.ts ~429-667), behind the context-status op, produces a GENERIC per-kind block summary for every block (status aggregation works for custom kinds), but special-cases 5 stock lifecycle blocks with hardcoded fields/status values: phase (status completed), requirements (byStatus + byPriority on field priority), tasks (byStatus), domain (entries total), verification (status passed/failed split). So a custom-vocabulary substrate gets only the generic status summary, not the declared lifecycle metrics the stock kinds enjoy.

evidence.reference: special-cased phase/requirements/tasks/domain/verification lifecycle metrics with hardcoded fields + status literals (completed, passed, failed)
generic per-array status-summary path (works for any kind) — the default custom kinds fall back to

impact: the lifecycle/planning projection in context-status is stock-vocabulary-specific; a custom substrate's lifecycle (its own phase/verification-equivalent kinds + status values) is not surfaced as structured metrics — only counted generically.
proposed_resolution: Make the lifecycle metrics config-declared (which kinds + which status values yield the lifecycle/metrics projection), keeping the generic per-kind summary as the default. Pairs with FGAP-017's state-derivation registry. Advances FEAT-004.
